notes.v1_behavior: Fields marked future_v2 are preserved in tasks.json but ignored by current import worker. They activate when import worker is updated.
notes.description_is_critical: In v1.0 the execution worker builds prompts almost entirely from the description field. Put everything useful there — requirement context, done condition, failure behavior, architectural constraints. This is the primary intelligence injection point until memory and decisions are connected to the prompt builder.

task_id.note: Sequential. Global across all scopes. TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003...

title.note: Action-based. Starts with a verb. Short and specific.

description.note: CRITICAL in v1.0. This is the primary content the execution worker uses to build the prompt. Must contain full context.

status.note: Always 'pending' on generation. Import worker changes this.

priority.note: Derived from requirement criticality. Blocking tasks are urgent. Dependencies are high.

action_type.note: Always claude_execute for feature tasks generated by the Gate.

prompt.note: The prompt the execution worker uses in AI mode. Same content as description but formatted as a direct instruction.

sqlite_project_slug.note: Must match an existing project slug in the SQLite projects table. Determines which project this task belongs to.

execution_mode.note: Always manual for Gate-generated tasks in v1.0. Triggers the operator note prepend in the import worker.

scope.note: Used now for organization and Section B output. Not yet read by import worker but used by the Operator for scope mapping.

sub_scope.note: Required for Tier 3 tasks. Null for Tier 1 and 2. Identifies which sub-scope within a large scope this task belongs to.

order.note: Global execution order across all scopes. Task-001 is order 1. Used by the Operator for scope map.

tier.note: Scope tier. 1 = fast lane (1-5 tasks), 2 = standard (6-15), 3 = phase work (16+, requires sub-scopes).

requirement_ref.note: Which requirement in the PRD this task satisfies. Every task must reference one requirement. Used by Operator for contract verification.

success_criteria.note: Derived directly from the OUTPUT element of the requirement contract. Must be testable and observable. Not 'it works' — specific observable outcome.

failure_behavior.note: Derived directly from the FAILURE PATH element of the requirement contract. What the code must do when this fails — retry logic, error state, fallback.

depends_on.note: Task IDs that must be complete before this task can start. Empty array if no dependencies.

description_template.note: Use this template to build every task description. In v1.0 this is the primary intelligence injection into the execution worker prompt.
description_template.format:
OBJECTIVE:
[Clear statement of what needs to be done — one to three sentences]

REQUIREMENT:
[requirement_ref]: [restate the full requirement contract in plain English]

ARCHITECTURAL CONSTRAINTS:
- [constraint from PRD that applies to this task]
- [constraint from PRD that applies to this task]

DONE WHEN:
[testable done condition derived from requirement OUTPUT element]

FAILURE BEHAVIOR:
[what the implementation must do when it fails — derived from requirement FAILURE PATH element]

DO NOT:
- [specific forbidden action from PRD or architecture]
- [specific forbidden action]

output_structure.note: tasks.json is a JSON array. Tasks are ordered by global execution order. Scopes are grouped — all tasks in scope A appear before tasks in scope B.
output_structure.scope_grouping: Tasks within the same scope appear consecutively ordered by their internal dependency sequence.
output_structure.sub_scope_grouping: For Tier 3 work, all tasks in sub-scope A appear before tasks in sub-scope B within the parent scope.

infrastructure_task_template.note: Infrastructure tasks are generated when a project requires capabilities the system does not yet have. They appear in a separate INFRASTRUCTURE scope before feature scopes.
examples: Infrastructure / Memory: Connect memory_service to execution worker prompt builder
Infrastructure / Decisions: Connect decision_service to execution worker prompt builder
Infrastructure / Schema: Add blueprints table migration
Infrastructure / Schema: Add session_logs table migration
Infrastructure / Dashboard: Add /api/memory endpoints to server.py
Infrastructure / Dashboard: Add /api/decisions endpoints to server.py
Infrastructure / Worker: Add memory entry ingestion to import worker
